books.title: 21 Lessons for the 21st Century
books.author: Yuval Noah Harari
books.description: It was the first book I read from start to finish that made me enjoy reading. The book discusses important issues humanity faces in the 21st century, such as artificial intelligence, climate change, social inequality, among others. The author presents a deep and thought-provoking analysis of the future of humanity and how we can prepare for it.

books.title: The Intellectual Life
books.author: Antonin-Dalmace Sertillanges
books.description: Must-read. A great book about the importance of the intellectual life, study, and the pursuit of knowledge, and how this can bring meaning and purpose to life. It is a somewhat dense read but timeless.

books.title: 10% Happier
books.author: Dan Harris
books.description: A very good book about meditation and mindfulness, written by a journalist who went through a near-death experience and discovered the practice as a way to deal with everyday stress and anxiety.

books.title: The Drunkard's Walk
books.author: Leonard Mlodinow
books.description: A fascinating book about the role of chance and randomness in our lives. It briefly tells the history of statistics/probability and how we often underestimate the influence of luck in our successes and failures. It helps develop a more realistic perspective on what we can control and what is unpredictable.

books.title: Thinking, Fast and Slow
books.author: Daniel Kahneman
books.description: A classic of psychology and behavioral economics, written by a Nobel Prize winner. The book explores the two systems of thought that govern our decisions: the fast, intuitive and emotional system, and the slow, deliberate and logical system. It is a dense/heavy read but describes very well how we think and make decisions.

books.title: Same as Ever
books.author: Morgan Housel
books.description: Talks about facts and human behaviors that remain constant over time.

books.title: Talking to Strangers
books.author: Malcolm Gladwell
books.description: A great book that explores how we interpret and communicate with people we don’t know, and the common mistakes we make when judging others. It can be somewhat heavy, but it is very interesting for understanding the complexity of human interactions.

books.title: Meditations
books.author: Marcus Aurelius
books.description: A classic of Stoic philosophy. The book is a journal by Marcus Aurelius, one of the greatest Roman emperors. Essential for anyone interested in Stoicism.

books.title: Enchiridion
books.author: Epictetus
books.description: A classic of Stoic philosophy, written by a former slave who became one of the greatest philosophers of his time. The book is a collection of teachings and reflections on how to live a virtuous and happy life, even in the face of adversity.

books.title: On the Shortness of Life
books.author: Seneca
books.description: A classic of Stoic philosophy, written by one of the greatest Roman philosophers. The book reflects on the fleeting nature of life and the importance of making the most of it by living according to virtue and reason.

books.title: The Daily Stoic
books.author: Ryan Holiday
books.description: Presents a collection of Stoic teachings organized in a daily journal format, with daily reflections and quotes from Stoic philosophers. It is a great practical introduction to Stoicism and can be read in small daily excerpts for inspiration and reflection.

books.title: Extreme Ownership: How U.S. Navy SEALs Lead and Win
books.author: Jocko Willink e Leif Babin
books.description: Written by two former Navy SEALs who share their experiences and lessons learned about leadership, teamwork, and discipline. The book gives practical examples applied in the military and in the corporate world.

books.title: Go and Win: Decoding the Elite Squad
books.author: Paulo Storani
books.description: Teaches how to build elite, high-performance teams based on the author’s experience as a former BOPE captain, with references to the movie Elite Squad.
